text.title: go-http-handlers Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for go-http-handlers as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: c.JSON(status, gin.H{"error": err.Error()}) for error responses.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Exposes database DSNs, constraint names, and internal hostnames. Use the RFC 7807 error mapping from error-handling/ and return via c.Error(appErr).

failure-mode.description: Forgetting defer r.Body.Close() in stdlib handlers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without Close(), the underlying TCP connection is not returned to the pool, leaking file descriptors under sustained load.

failure-mode.description: Creating validator.New() per request instead of once at package level.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: validator.New() is expensive (reflection over all struct tags). A per-request instance adds measurable latency under load. Cache at package or handler-struct level.

failure-mode.description: Using path param accessor from the wrong router: c.Param("id") in Chi or r.PathValue("id") in Gin.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each router has its own path-param API. Wrong accessor returns an empty string, silently breaking 404 logic and tests.
